summary: Balanced matrix mix that captures the legendary May‑1977 Cornell show with crystal‑clear Jerry solos and a barn‑burning setlist.
review: The three‑source matrix created by Dan delivers a surprisingly clear, well‑balanced sound that lets listeners hear every instrument – even Jerry’s soaring lead tones – while still preserving enough audience ambience to feel the energy of the night.  Musically the performance is hailed as a "barn‑burner" with standout moments on Brown Eyed Women, Dancin’ in the Streets, Scarlet > Fire and an epic Not Fade Away/Stephen suite; only occasional phasing glitches are noted.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (majority 5‑star) praise both the musical performance and the matrix mix; only a few note phase issues, so the combined show‑plus‑recording score is strong.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally praised as balanced, clear and crisp. A handful of reviewers report slight out‑of‑phase or sync problems (vocals, hi‑hats) and that the audience level is modest, but these are considered minor compared with the overall fidelity.

standout_songs: Not Fade Away > St. Stephen > Not Fade Away
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set rolls with classic 1977 energy – tight transitions, powerful second‑set jams and a seamless flow that keeps the crowd moving from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers call his solos on Brown Eyed Women, Jack Straw and The Deal "beyond ridiculous" and note his voice sits perfectly in the mix.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for a solid, thumping bass especially on Fire; several fans say you can hear every note clearly.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for funky rhythm work and driving energy throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s piano/organ is highlighted on Morning Dew and Scarlet > Fire; occasional comments about slightly low keyboard presence in the matrix mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (and later Mickey) receive compliments for tight, propulsive drumming that underpins the high‑energy jams.
